entries.definition:
كتاب النون

نكل

(نكح) النون والكاف والحاء أصلٌ واحد، وهو البِضاع. ونَكَحَ يَنْكِحُ. وامرأةٌ ناكِحٌ في بني فلان، أي ذات زَوجٍ منهم. والنِّكاح يكون العَقدَ دونَ الوطء. يقال نَكَحْتُ: تَزَوّجْتُ. وأنكَحْتُ غَيرِي.

نَكَثَ nakatha u [v. trans.] to break an agreement, to go back on
a promise, to break one’s word (43:50) فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمْ
يَنْكُثُونَ but when We removed from them the torment, they went
back on their word.

أَنْكَاث ʾankāth [pl. of n. نِكْث nikth] untwisted pieces of yarn (a
state of complete disarray, complete disintegration) (16:92) وَلَا
تَكُونُوا كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَهَا مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ أَنْكَاثًا do not be like a woman who
unravels her yarn into fibres, after it has become strong, [falling
thus into complete disintegration/losing your unity].
entries.definition_english:
نَكَثَ nakatha u [v. trans.] to break an agreement, to go back on
a promise, to break one’s word (43:50) فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذَابَ إِذَا هُمْ
يَنْكُثُونَ but when We removed from them the torment, they went
back on their word.

أَنْكَاث ʾankāth [pl. of n. نِكْث nikth] untwisted pieces of yarn (a
state of complete disarray, complete disintegration) (16:92) وَلَا
تَكُونُوا كَالَّتِي نَقَضَتْ غَزْلَهَا مِنْ بَعْدِ قُوَّةٍ أَنْكَاثًا do not be like a woman who
unravels her yarn into fibres, after it has become strong, [falling
thus into complete disintegration/losing your unity].

entries.definition:
n–k–ḥ to marry, to be married, to give in matrimony; to
fornicate, fornication; to drench the land (with rain), to be
overcome (by sleep). Of this root, four forms occur 23 times in
the Qur’an: نَكَحَ nakaha 14 times; أَنْكَحَ ʾankaha three times; اِسْتَنْكَحَ
ʾistankaha once and نِكَاح nikāḥ four times.

نَكَحَ nakaha i [v. trans.] 1 to wed, to marry someone (4:22) وَلَا
تَنْكِحُوا مَا نَكَحَ آبَاؤُكُمْ مِنَ النِّسَاءِ do not marry any of the women that
your fathers married 2 (possibly, in an interpretation of 24:3) to
fornicate, to copulate with, to have intercourse with الزَّانِي لَا يَنْكِحُ إِلَّا
زَانِيَةً أَوْ مُشْرِكَةً وَالزَّانِيَةُ لَا يَنْكِحُهَا إِلَّا زَانٍ أَوْ مُشْرِكٌ the adulterer only
fornicates with an adulteress or an idolatress, and the adultress
only fornicates with an adulterer or an idolater.

أُنْكِحَ ʾunkiḥa [imperf. v. IV] I [trans.] to marry off, to enable to
marry, to facilitate the marriage of (24:32) وَأَنْكِحُوا الْأَيَامَى مِنْكُمْ marry
off the single among you II [doubly trans.] to give to someone in
marriage (28:27) إِنِّي أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُنْكِحَكَ إِحْدَى ابْنَتَيَّ هَاتَيْنِ I wish to give you
in marriage one of these two daughters of mine.

اِسْتَنْكَحَ ʾistankaha [v. X trans.] to seek to marry, to desire to
marry (33:50) إِنْ أَرَادَ النَّبِيُّ أَنْ يَسْتَنْكِحَهَا if the Prophet wishes to seek
her in marriage.

نِكَاح nikāḥ [n.] marriage (also marriage cost) (24:33) وَلْيَسْتَعْفِفِ
الَّذِينَ لَا يَجِدُونَ نِكَاحًا let those who are unable to afford marriage keep
chaste.
